fix(pdf): atomic re-render with overlay + debounced zoom

Problems fixed:
1. Pages disappeared during zoom — reRenderPages() was removing all page
   elements then rendering one-by-one, causing a blank flash each zoom.
   Fix: render all new pages into an off-screen temp container first, then
   atomically swap (insert before overlay, remove overlay last) so the
   user always sees either the old pages or all new pages — never blank.

2. Rapid scroll events queued many concurrent re-renders — each wheel
   event triggered a full reRenderPages() call immediately.
   Fix: debounce re-render 250ms after the last wheel event. The zoom
   label updates immediately on every tick for responsive feedback; only
   the expensive canvas re-render is deferred.

3. Initial load showed pages one-by-one after removing the spinner.
   Fix: render all pages into temp container while spinner is visible,
   remove spinner and insert all pages atomically in one frame.

CSS:
- via-pdf-scroll gains position:relative for overlay to anchor to
- via-pdf-loading-overlay: position:absolute inset:0 z-index:50 covers
  existing pages during re-render

Bump: 0.1.6 → 0.1.7

commit.message:
email: implement context cancellation check in Send (#180)

The Send method's doc comment claimed it respects context cancellation
during the dial phase, but the implementation ignored the context
parameter entirely (using _ as the parameter name).

Fix this by:
1. Checking ctx.Err() before initiating the SMTP send
2. Updating the doc comment to accurately describe behavior
3. Using the named ctx parameter instead of _

Added tests:
- TestSend_CancelledContext: verifies cancelled context returns error
- TestSend_DeadlineExceeded: verifies expired deadline returns error

Co-authored-by: devaloi <devaloi@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: prevent concurrent sessions from overwriting other users' threads

Add merge-on-write (saveMemoryMerged) to all hooks that write sticky-note.json.
Before saving, re-reads the file from disk and preserves any threads by ID that
exist on disk but not in memory. This prevents one user's session-end from
silently dropping another user's threads.

Replace fragile merge=union gitattribute with a custom JSON-aware git merge
driver that does a proper 3-way merge of threads by ID. For threads present
in both sides, the version with the latest last_activity_at wins. Threads
unique to either side are preserved.

The merge driver is installed to .sticky-note/merge-driver.js by both init
and update commands, with git configured via merge.sticky-note.driver.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

database: return joined error when both fn and rollback fail in WithTx (#177)

Previously, when a transaction function returned an error and the
subsequent rollback also failed, only the original fn error was returned
to the caller while the rollback error was logged. This made it
impossible for callers to programmatically detect rollback failures.

Now use errors.Join to return both errors, preserving the original
behavior of logging the rollback error while also making it available
to the caller via errors.Is/errors.As.

fix: discard stale poll results after network switch

Added a generation counter (AtomicU64) that increments on every
network switch. Spawned poll tasks capture the current generation
and check it before sending results — if the generation changed
(network switched while in-flight), results are silently dropped
to prevent cross-network data contamination.

* Feature/aviationstack primary (#13)

* feat(worker): switch primary API to AviationStack with flight_date + 30d KV cache

- Remove AirLabs dependency; AviationStack is now the sole upstream API
- Add flight_date param to enable historical flight queries (key use case:
  pilots fill logbook after landing when flight is no longer 'live')
- Extend KV TTL from 7d to 30d to protect the 500 req/month free quota
- Worker acts as HTTPHTTPS proxy: App always uses HTTPS to reach Worker,
  Worker calls AviationStack via plain http:// (free-tier limitation),
  bypassing iOS ATS and Android Cleartext Traffic restrictions
- Add X-Cache: HIT/MISS response header for cache verification
- Validate date param format (YYYY-MM-DD) at Worker level
- Rename env secret AVIATIONSTACK_KEY  AVIATIONSTACK_API_KEY
- No App-side changes needed: ApiService.ts date format already YYYY-MM-DD

* fix(worker): replace broken ICAO slice with native flight_icao param + add limit=1

Bug: CCA1501  slice(0,2)+slice(3)  CC1501 (Macair), causing 100% miss
     for all Chinese 3-letter ICAO designators (CCA/CES/CSN etc.)

Fix: detect IATA(2-letter) vs ICAO(3-letter) via regex and dynamically
     choose flight_iata vs flight_icao AviationStack param  no string
     manipulation, AviationStack handles the lookup natively.

Also: add &limit=1 to minimise JSON payload size and response latency.

* fix(worker): use https:// for AviationStack per official docs (Basic Plan+)

Official API docs clearly state base URL is https://api.aviationstack.com/v1/
The previous http:// assumption was based on outdated free-tier reports.
Basic Plan and above supports HTTPS natively  full end-to-end TLS now.

* security: remove plaintext API key from wrangler.toml

Key must be stored as an encrypted Cloudflare Secret, not a [vars] entry.
Use: wrangler secret put AVIATIONSTACK_API_KEY

commit.message:
feat: implement PDF viewer with zoom and freehand annotations

Registers a custom FileView for .pdf files using pdfjs-dist v3 (CJS).

Key implementation details:
- PDF.js worker inlined as a blob URL at build time (via esbuild plugin)
  to work around Electron's renderer-process worker restrictions
- All pages rendered as flex column with correct sizing for mixed
  orientation documents (portrait + landscape)
- Loading spinner shown while pdfjs parses the document
- Annotation canvas overlaid on each page; tools: pen, highlighter, eraser
- Annotation coordinates stored normalised (0-1) so they survive zoom
- Zoom controls: step buttons + percentage label (25–400%, 9 snap levels)
  click label to reset to 100%
- Annotations persisted to companion .annotations.json sidecar file
- Intersection-observer-based visible page detection for clear-page action
